title_en: Symptoms of Jinn Possession and Evil Eye
content_en:
Bali devotes considerable attention to the practical question of identifying spiritual affliction — a matter of significant importance both for proper diagnosis and for protecting vulnerable individuals from exploitation by charlatans. He surveys the scholarly literature on the symptoms associated with jinn possession, the evil eye (al-'ayn), and sihr (black magic), while consistently emphasizing that physical and psychological explanations must be ruled out before spiritual causes are assumed.

The evil eye is attested in numerous authentic hadiths. The Prophet said: 'The evil eye is real' (Muslim), and he instructed his Companions in methods of protection against it. The evil eye occurs when a person with a particularly powerful and envious gaze — or sometimes even an admiring one without remembering Allah — inadvertently transmits a harmful influence. Classical scholars explain this through the concept that the human soul can project forces, for good or ill, through the eyes, and that this projection is a real metaphysical reality even if its precise mechanism is unknown to us.

Symptoms traditionally associated with the evil eye include sudden unexplained illness following someone's praise or admiration, a general sense of malaise or weakness, and disruption in affairs that were previously proceeding smoothly. Bali is careful to note that these symptoms are not diagnostic by themselves; many illnesses produce similar presentations. The presence of the evil eye should be suspected particularly when the timing of illness correlates with someone's particular attention, and when medical examination fails to identify a physical cause.

Jinn possession presents with a somewhat different profile. Bali surveys reports from practitioners of ruqyah and from classical texts to identify common indicators: extreme aversion to Quranic recitation or the call to prayer, unusual physical strength during episodes, loss of consciousness followed by complete memory loss of the period, and manifestation of a distinctly different 'personality' during episodes. The scholarly tradition consistently warns against hasty diagnosis of possession, as many of these symptoms can have psychological or neurological causes that require medical treatment.

Black magic (sihr) is condemned in the Quran as an act of disbelief (2:102), and its effects are real by scholarly consensus. The Prophet himself was affected by sihr at one point, and Allah revealed Surah al-Falaq and Surah an-Nas partly as a remedy. Common symptoms attributed to sihr include disruption of marriages, inability to engage in intimacy, persistent nightmares, inexplicable hatred between people who previously loved each other, and obstruction of livelihood despite reasonable effort.

The chapter concludes with important warnings: a good Muslim should avoid obsessing over spiritual affliction, as excessive preoccupation can itself be a form of waswas. The correct posture is to take sensible precautions (regular prayers, morning and evening adhkar, recitation of Ayat al-Kursi), seek both medical and scholarly help when symptoms are genuinely concerning, and maintain trust in Allah's protection for those who truly rely upon Him.
content_id:
Bali memberikan perhatian yang cukup besar pada pertanyaan praktis tentang mengidentifikasi gangguan spiritual, suatu hal yang sangat penting baik untuk diagnosis yang tepat maupun untuk melindungi individu-individu yang rentan dari eksploitasi oleh para penipu. Ia mensurvei literatur ilmiah tentang gejala-gejala yang dikaitkan dengan kerasukan jin, ain (pandangan hasad), dan sihr (sihir), sambil secara konsisten menekankan bahwa penjelasan fisik dan psikologis harus disingkirkan terlebih dahulu sebelum penyebab spiritual diasumsikan.

Ain dibuktikan dalam banyak hadis sahih. Nabi bersabda: Ain itu nyata (HR. Muslim), dan beliau mengajari para sahabatnya cara-cara perlindungan dari ain. Ain terjadi ketika seseorang dengan tatapan yang sangat kuat dan penuh hasad, atau terkadang bahkan tatapan yang mengagumi tanpa menyebut nama Allah, tanpa sengaja menularkan pengaruh yang berbahaya. Para ulama klasik menjelaskan hal ini melalui konsep bahwa jiwa manusia dapat memproyeksikan kekuatan, baik yang baik maupun yang buruk, melalui mata, dan bahwa proyeksi ini merupakan realitas metafisik yang nyata meskipun mekanisme pastinya tidak diketahui oleh kita.

Gejala-gejala yang secara tradisional dikaitkan dengan ain mencakup sakit mendadak yang tidak dapat dijelaskan setelah seseorang memuji atau mengagumi, perasaan umum tidak enak badan atau lemah, dan gangguan pada urusan-urusan yang sebelumnya berjalan lancar. Bali secara cermat mencatat bahwa gejala-gejala ini tidak bersifat diagnostik dengan sendirinya. Banyak penyakit menghasilkan presentasi yang serupa. Kehadiran ain harus dicurigai khususnya ketika waktu sakit berkorelasi dengan perhatian khusus dari seseorang, dan ketika pemeriksaan medis gagal mengidentifikasi penyebab fisik.

Kerasukan jin menampilkan profil yang agak berbeda. Bali mensurvei laporan dari para praktisi ruqyah dan dari teks-teks klasik untuk mengidentifikasi indikator-indikator umum: keengganan yang ekstrem terhadap bacaan Al-Qur’an atau azan, kekuatan fisik yang tidak biasa selama episode, kehilangan kesadaran diikuti oleh hilangnya ingatan sepenuhnya selama periode tersebut, dan manifestasi kepribadian yang secara jelas berbeda selama episode. Tradisi ilmiah secara konsisten memperingatkan terhadap diagnosis kerasukan yang tergesa-gesa, karena banyak dari gejala-gejala ini dapat memiliki penyebab psikologis atau neurologis yang memerlukan pengobatan medis.

Sihr dikutuk dalam Al-Qur’an sebagai tindakan kekafiran (2:102), dan efeknya nyata berdasarkan konsensus ulama. Nabi sendiri pernah terkena sihr pada suatu waktu, dan Allah menurunkan Surah al-Falaq dan Surah an-Nas sebagian sebagai obat. Gejala-gejala umum yang dikaitkan dengan sihr mencakup gangguan pernikahan, ketidakmampuan untuk berhubungan intim, mimpi buruk yang terus-menerus, kebencian yang tidak dapat dijelaskan antara orang-orang yang sebelumnya saling mencintai, dan hambatan dalam mencari penghidupan meskipun telah berupaya dengan wajar.

Bab ini ditutup dengan peringatan-peringatan penting: seorang Muslim yang baik harus menghindari terobsesi dengan gangguan spiritual, karena keasyikan yang berlebihan dapat sendirinya menjadi bentuk waswas. Sikap yang benar adalah mengambil tindakan pencegahan yang wajar (shalat rutin, zikir pagi dan petang, membaca Ayat Kursi), mencari bantuan medis maupun ulama ketika gejala-gejala benar-benar mengkhawatirkan, dan mempertahankan kepercayaan pada perlindungan Allah bagi mereka yang sungguh-sungguh bertawakal kepada-Nya.
content_ar: Bali devotes considerable attention to the practical question of identifying spiritual affliction — a matter of المهم importance both for proper diagnosis and for protecting vulnerable individuals from exploitation by charlatans. He surveys the العلمي literature on the symptoms associated with الجن possession, the الشر eye (al-'ayn), and sihr (black magic), بينما consistently emphasizing that physical and psychological explanations must be ruled out قبل spiritual causes are assumed. The الشر eye is attested in numerous authentic hadiths. النبي ﷺ said: 'The الشر eye is real' (مسلم), and he instructed his الصحابة in methods of protection against it. The الشر eye occurs عندما a person with a particularly powerful and envious gaze — or sometimes even an admiring one without remembering الله — inadvertently transmits a harmful influence. الكلاسيكي العلماء explain this through the concept that the human النفس can project forces, for الخير or ill, through the eyes, and that this projection is a real metaphysical reality even إذا its precise mechanism is unknown to us. Symptoms traditionally associated with the الشر eye include sudden unexplained المرض following someone's praise or admiration, a general sense of malaise or الضعف, and disruption in affairs that were previously proceeding smoothly. Bali is careful to note that these symptoms are not diagnostic by themselves; many illnesses produce similar presentations. The presence of the الشر eye should be suspected particularly عندما the timing of المرض correlates with someone's particular attention, and عندما medical examination fails to identify a physical cause. الجن possession presents with a somewhat different profile. Bali surveys reports from practitioners of ruqyah and from الكلاسيكي texts to identify common indicators: extreme aversion to القرآني التلاوة or the call to الصلاة, unusual physical القوة خلال episodes, loss of consciousness followed by complete memory loss of the period, and manifestation of a distinctly different 'personality' خلال episodes. The العلمي التراث consistently warns against hasty diagnosis of possession, as many of these symptoms can have psychological or neurological causes that require medical treatment. Black magic (sihr) is condemned in the القرآن الكريم as an act of disbelief (2:102), and its effects are real by العلمي consensus. النبي ﷺ himself was affected by sihr at one point, and الله revealed السورة al-Falaq and السورة an-Nas partly as a remedy.
